terms.definition: The degree to which the component parts of a social system act in mutually reinforcing alignment toward shared norms and goals.
Annual number of divorces per 1,000 persons in the population.
Average number of live births per woman over her lifetime, adjusted for cohort.
A non-optional environmental parameter that shapes behavioral equilibria within a system.
Percentage of households owning at least one radio receiver.
Share of adults attending religious services on a weekly basis.
Legal dissolution of marriage that does not require proof of wrongdoing by either party.
Scalar measure (0–1) of income or wealth inequality; 0 signifies perfect equality.
Population-level increase in social isolation with measurable negative health outcomes.
Mathematical model describing how a technology spreads through a population over time, characterized by an S-shaped curve.
